title: Train Route Atlas
description: Twelve legendary train routes — high-speed, scenic, night, and classic — draped over an interactive 3D globe with specs, filters, and popups, all client-side.

title: Geopuesto Playground
description: Spherical-geometry experiments for great circles, equidistant points, polyhedra, and curve analysis on a globe.

title: U.S. Fantasy Transit Network
description: A generated four-tier North American transit network connecting Census places at continental, regional, metro, and commuter scales.

title: Concord Civil War
description: An alternate-history scrollytelling map that divides Concord, New Hampshire along the Merrimack River.

title: Bug Wars
description: A compact browser strategy game about building an insect colony and surviving rival swarms.

title: Flip Game
description: A bottle-flip physics game — time your flick, land the bottle, chain streaks across increasingly absurd targets.

title: Grog Flip
description: The pirate-parrot cousin of Flip Game, built for the Whydah unit — flip Grog's tankard and stick the landing.

title: True Scale
description: A map experiment for comparing the apparent and actual size of places across latitudes.

title: Whydah Voyage
description: A project-based learning dashboard for a 5-week middle-school unit on the Whydah Gally — the 1717 pirate ship wrecked off Cape Cod — with maps, a navigator, handouts, and daily pages.

title: Tappymaps Arcade
description: Tap-to-color US state and county maps plus a shelf of geography arcade games — locate, recall, duel, and daily draft modes.

title: MCAS Item Dashboard
description: An interactive catalog of released MCAS math items — browse by year, standard, and difficulty, with item images and figures.

title: Savvas Curriculum Extraction
description: Work-in-progress viewer for the Savvas curriculum extraction — course, topic, and section structure as it comes out of the parser.

title: Predicting Tomorrow's Housing Crisis
description: An interactive tract explorer for a predictive GIS suitability model — housing stress, homelessness pressure, and supportive-housing suitability across 1,613 Massachusetts census tracts, computed in open-source Python.

title: Sequential Interstate Challenge
description: Drive every two-digit Interstate end to end — an optimized order and orientation cuts connector drive time 86% versus strict numerical order, on an interactive A-vs-B map.

title: Mapzimus Board
description: The lab's idea engine — a live board that scores roughly 20,000 candidate map ideas by predicted virality with a weighted seven-dimension V-Score.
